details.body:
Nuclear Waste Services

Nuclear Waste Services (NWS) is a trading name of both LLW Repository Limited (LLWR) and Radioactive Waste Management Limited (RWM).

NWS is a division of the Nuclear Decommissioning Authority (NDA) which includes RWM and LLWR. NWS is not a legal entity but provides strategic oversight over the operation and development of these businesses through a management board governance structure.

Make a new request to LLWR or RWM by contacting us using the details below.

Freedom of Information

Email FOIrequest@nuclearwasteservices.uk

Nuclear Waste Services Limited (NWS Ltd) is a subsidiary of the Nuclear Decommissioning Authority (NDA).

If the information you want is not routinely published, you can make a request for it under the Freedom of Information Act.

LLW Repository Ltd

Who are we what we do

  • LLW Repository Board
  • Site Locations and Contacts

What we spend and how we spend it

Costs

LLW Repository Ltd is a wholly owned subsidiary of the NDA.

NDA is a non-departmental public body sponsored by the Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, and publishes a tax strategy for the NDA Group in accordance with schedule 19 of the Finance Act 2016.

As a result, LLW Repository Ltd does not publish a tax strategy.

Information on expenditure, including income statements and financial reports can be found at Companies House.

Latest LLW Repository spend data including expenses paid to staff and travel card spend is available on request foirequest@nuclearwasteservices.uk.

LLW Repository Ltd Grading Structure

Procurement and Tendering Procedures

LLW Repository Ltd procurement information is available here

What are our priorities and how we’re doing

  • The LLWR Plan

  • LLW Repository Ltd Social Impact plan

  • LLW Repository Ltd Small Medium Enterprise action plan

Our policies and procedures

It is our intent to publish a range of additional policies and procedures in this section as we move forward, covering issues such as corporate governance, health and safety and financial management.

Policy and procedures for the provision of service and conduct of business

  • LLWR Gender Pay Gap Report

  • Personal Information Charter

  • Sustainability Policy

  • Accessible Documents Policy

  • EHS&Q Policy

  • Business Continuity Policy

  • Risk Management Policy

  • Security in the Supply Chain Policy

  • Procurement and Supply Chain Policy

  • Cyber Security and Information Assurance Policy

  • IS&T Policy

  • Knowledge Management Policy

  • Asset Management Policy

  • Security Policy

  • Information Risk Management Policy

Policy and procedures for the recruitment and employment of employees

  • Modern Slavery and Human Trafficking Policy

  • Mental Health and Well Being Policy

  • Equality Policy

  • Travel Safety Policy

  • Respect at Work Policy

NDA Group Documents

  • NDA Annual Report and Accounts 2020-2021

  • NDA Business Plan

  • NDA Strategy Stakeholder Report 2020-2021

List and registers

Register of directors’ interests will be provided shortly.

LLW Repository Ltd uses CCTV for the purpose of crime prevention/detection, employee safety and the protection of property on and immediately around all of its sites.

CCTV surveillance is controlled by: Nuclear Decommissioning Authority/LLW Repository Ltd.

A register of gifts and hospitality provided to board members and senior personnel is available on request from foirequest@nuclearwasteservices.uk.

Any registers of interests kept by the company

LLW Repository Ltd has no interest in any other companies.

Radioactive Waste Management Ltd

Who we are and what we do

You can find our contact information on our home page

Roles and responsibilities

  • You can find out about our roles and responsibilities
  • Our Board and Executive Team
  • Our organisation chart

What we spend and how we spend it

Costs

  • Information on our expenditure is held within our RWM Annual Report and Accounts 2019 to 2020 (PDF, 5.69 MB, 24 pages)

  • Spend data – invoices paid to contractors and expenses paid to staff
  • Our pay scales and grading structure are held in RWM roles and salary ranges April 2020 (PDF, 106 KB, 3 pages)

Procurement and tendering procedures

RWM follows the procedures that have been developed by NDA. All suppliers of goods and services to the RWM should familiarise themselves with our Terms and Conditions.

  • General Terms and Condition of Contract for Suppliers or Contractors

  • General Terms and Conditions of Contract - Purchase of Goods and Services

  • Information security responsibilities for contractors handling official documents outside NDA premises

  • Contractors working in non-NDA locations

  • Cyber Essentials in the NDA

  • Details of our approach to procurement

  • Lists of contract opportunities and awards with details of value are available via Contracts Finder

What our priorities are and how we are doing

RWM’s priorities

  • Corporate Strategy 2019
  • Health, Safety, Security, Safeguards, Environment and Quality Strategy
  • Science and Technology Plan
  • Geological Disposal Facility Issues Register

How RWM is doing

  • Annual Report and Financial Statements 2019 to 2020 (PDF, 440 KB, 36 pages)
  • Our disposability assessment customer feedback

How we make decisions

The minutes of the RWM Board

Our policies and procedures

Policies and procedures for the provision of services and conduct of business:

You can find the following below.

  • RWM Information Governance Policy (PDF, 716 KB, 5 pages)
  • RWM Health, Safety, Security, Environmental and Quality (HSSEQ) Policy (PDF, 6.15 MB, 4 pages)

RWM has adopted NDA’s policies in the following areas:

  • Intellectual Property Policy

Policies and procedures for the recruitment and employment of staff:

RWM has adopted NDA’s policies in these areas:

  • Equal Opportunities, Harassment, Discrimination and Diversity Policy
  • Anti-Fraud, Bribery and Corruption Policy

You can find the following policies below:

  • RWM Health, Safety, Security, Environmental and Quality (HSSEQ) Policy (PDF, 6.15 MB, 4 pages)
  • RWM Travel and Expenses Policy (PDF, 776 KB, 11 pages)
  • RWM Whistleblowing Policy (PDF, 238 KB, 3 pages)

Policies and procedures for customer service

You can find the following policies below:

  • RWM Customer Complaints Policy (PDF, 128 KB, 5 pages)
  • RWM Processing requests for information (PDF, 798 KB, 14 pages)
  • RWM Internal Review of Information Request Complaints (PDF, 145 KB, 10 pages)
  • RWM Whistleblowing Policy (PDF, 238 KB, 3 pages)

Policies and procedures for records management and personal data:

You can find the following policies below:

  • RWM Information Governance Policy (PDF, 716 KB, 5 pages)

Lists and registers

  • Register of gifts and hospitality - available on request
  • Register of Directors’ interests - available on request
  • RWM Information Asset Register - available on request

The services we offer

  • You can find out about our work with radioactive waste producers
  • Our recent announcements

Fees

NWS does not charge for the provision of information listed in the scheme. NWS retains the right to charge for the written requests in line with the SI2004/3244 The Freedom of Information and Data Protection (Appropriate Limit and Fees) Regulations 2004.

Written Requests

Information held by a public authority that is not published under this scheme can be requested in writing. Its provision will be considered in accordance with the provisions of the Freedom of Information Act. Please contact the NWS Freedom of Information Manager using the following contact details:

Freedom of Information

Email FOIrequest@nuclearwasteservices.uk

Nuclear Waste Services Limited (NWS Ltd) is a subsidiary of the Nuclear Decommissioning Authority (NDA).
